blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: Shirone…….
Emi clung to Shirone's hand.
Her eyes were swollen from crying.
No one came to check on them.
Or perhaps they had already accepted that Shirone was dead.

blocks.speaker: Emi
blocks.content: She couldn't accept that Shirone was dead.
There were too many inexplicable details for it to be mere obsession.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Senior, don't worry. Shirone will come back.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Exactly! It doesn't make sense. Why would he die? If mass teleportation was fatal, it shouldn't have worked in the first place. Something strange must have happened to Shirone.
Iruki frowned.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I don't care either way. Dead is dead. There's no room for emotions here.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: No. It's different.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Because right now, you're crying with such a sorrowful expression.
Iruki couldn't respond.
As Arin had said, before the mirror, a hundred words were meaningless.
The infirmary door slowly creaked open.
Emi had cried herself to exhaustion on a cot.
While Nade dozed in a chair in the corner.
Iruki approached Shirone.
Sleeping and death felt different from the start.
Seeing Shirone's cold, lifeless face made his heart ache.
By morning, the faculty would come to inspect Shirone.
They would prioritize embalming him for the funeral, but once that happened, there would be no way to bring him back.
Suppressing his emotions, Iruki pulled a scalpel from his pocket.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I"ll definitely save you.
He checked on Emi and Nade, but neither showed signs of waking.
After two days of extreme stress, it was only natural.
Finally, Iruki pressed the scalpel against Shirone's skin.
The blade glinted in the moonlight as it pierced Shirone's flesh.
